policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-008, CS-RULE-011, CS-RULE-012, CS-RULE-014, CS-RULE-015, FIN-RULE-001, FIN-RULE-004, FIN-RULE-005, FIN-RULE-010, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021]
expected_outcome: Customer disputes a $340 erroneous installation charge — over Daniel's $200 cap. Daniel CORRECTLY opens a Manager Approval ticket (FIN-RULE-005 PASS) but uses the forbidden phrase 'that is our policy' (CS-RULE-012 FAIL), skips Acknowledge in A.C.E.S. (CS-RULE-011 FAIL), and interrupts the customer twice (CS-RULE-008 FAIL). Mixed outcome — ticket opened, customer leaves partially satisfied but behaviour is coachable.
content_warning: Agent uses forbidden phrase; customer becomes angry mid-call.

coverage.element: Skips Acknowledge in A.C.E.S. (jumps to Solve)
Over agent cap — does NOT promise amount
Ticket SLA (Revenue Assurance 4 hrs / Manager 1 BD)

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Daniel. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: This call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Tom Reeves. Listen, I just got my bill and there's a three hundred and forty dollar charge on there that has no business being on there. I want it off.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay, Mr. Reeves. I can look into that for you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling regarding a billing charge. Before I pull anything up I need to verify your account. Can you give me your account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Nine-one-eight-three, four-four-two-two, seven-seven.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Last four of the payment method on file.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Six-two-one-five.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Full name on the account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Thomas Edward Reeves.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You're verified.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good. Now about the charge.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Pulling up the bill now.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Three forty. Labelled installation fee. I've been a customer for eleven years. Nothing was installed. Nobody came out.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Right, so I see the line item. The system shows an installation charge was added on the eighth of this month.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: On the eighth of this month nothing happened. I was at work. No tech came to my house. I didn't order any new equipment.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Let me check what work order is associated with it.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I don't care what work order — there shouldn't be one. I'm telling you nothing was —

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand, Mr. Reeves, I'm just looking at what the system is showing me.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You interrupted me. I was trying to tell you something.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: My apologies. Go ahead.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I was saying — nothing was installed. So how is there an installation fee? That's the question. And why is it my problem to figure out?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Well, when the system auto-generates a fee, that is our policy that the line item appears until it's reversed. I'm not saying it's correct, I'm just explaining the system.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That is our policy. Are you serious? That's exactly the kind of thing I hate hearing. That's not an answer.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I didn't mean it that way. Let me just check the work order.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay, I see — the work order references a fiber drop install at a different address than yours. So it looks like —

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Different address? You charged me for someone else's install?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's what it looks like. It's a billing error, and it's eligible for full reversal under our refund policy.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Thank you. Finally.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Now — I do need to be upfront about something. The amount is three hundred and forty dollars, which is above my single-call authorization cap of two hundred. I can't apply the reversal directly myself.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You've got to be kidding me. So now I have to wait?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm not going to quote you a number I can't authorize. What I'm going to do is open a Manager Approval ticket for the full reversal — that's what the policy requires for amounts over my cap. You will receive an outcome within one business day.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: One business day.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: One business day. Because this is also a clear billing error tied to a wrong address, I'm also opening a Revenue Assurance ticket so they investigate how it routed to your account. That team follows up within four hours.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fine. And when the credit is approved, how long until it's actually off my bill?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: The balance on your online account will update within five days but will officially appear on your next PDF bill.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Alright.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: a three hundred and forty dollar installation charge from a different address was incorrectly applied to your account. I'm opening a Manager Approval ticket for the full reversal with outcome within one business day, and a Revenue Assurance ticket to investigate the routing error with follow-up within four hours. Once approved, the credit will show on your next bill.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No. Just get the credit done.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number is N-X-L dash nine-nine-zero-four-five-two-one. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up. Thank you for being a NexaLink customer.
